message:
fix(web): correct keyboard sequence handling for multi-key shortcuts

The previous sequence buffer logic had a bug where typing an unrelated
key followed by the start of a valid sequence would fail to match.
For example, with shortcut "g h", typing "x" then "g h" would not
trigger the shortcut because the buffer contained ["x", "g", "h"]
instead of being reset to start fresh at "g".

Changes:
- Restructure the keydown handler to first check all sequences
  for full or partial matches against the updated buffer
- On full match: execute handler and clear buffer immediately
- On partial match: keep buffer and set/reset timeout
- On no match: check if current key starts any sequence, and if so
  start a new buffer with just that key
- Only check single-key shortcuts if no sequence activity occurred

This ensures sequences work reliably regardless of prior keypresses,
matching user expectations for vim-style multi-key shortcuts like
"g h" (go home) or "g p" (go to prompts).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
